signals.raw: **IMF Article IV Consultation (April 2, 2026)**: Confirmed U.S. GDP growth at 2.0% for 2025, projecting 2.4% in 2026; core PCE inflation at 2.9% in 2025, forecasted to 2.6% by end-2026 due to tariffs pushing goods prices higher (offset by cooling services); limited Fed rate cuts (fed funds from 3.6% to 3.4%); tariffs at 7-8.5% effective rate dragging growth and risking spillovers**[1].
